Semantic alignment is moderate as both address PySpark testing needs but through different execution strategies. Structural approaches are completely different - delegation vs direct implementation. Correctness varies significantly with delegation being syntactically simple but incomplete, while implementation is comprehensive but contains some technical issues.

Test_Implementation_Agent delivers exactly what was requested with detailed test cases covering positive/negative scenarios, boundary conditions, and performance testing (lines 15-180). However, semantic alignment is reduced because delegation doesn't provide actual deliverables, only task handoff.

Test_Implementation_Agent uses comprehensive implementation structure: test case table, detailed Pytest scripts with fixtures, multiple test functions, and execution templates (lines 15-200+). No structural overlap beyond both being text-based outputs.

Average of individual correctness scores (85 + 75) / 2 = 80. Both outputs are syntactically valid but Test_Implementation_Agent has more technical complexity and corresponding technical issues.
Consider implementing basic validation or preliminary analysis before delegation to ensure task feasibility and resource availability. Add estimated effort and timeline to delegation request.
